layers.nm: The lonely runner conjecture states that each runner will end up lonely at some point, no matter what speeds everyone runs at. It has now been proved for up to 10 runners.

s.t: The lonely runner conjecture states that each runner will end up lonely at some point, no matter what speeds everyone runs at. It has now been proved for up to 10 runners.

layers.nm: Three runners line up at the same position on a circular track that's one unit in length. They each start running at a unique, constant speed.

s.t: Three runners line up at the same position on a circular track that's one unit in length. They each start running at a unique, constant speed.

layers.nm: A runner is “lonely” if their distance from each of the other runners is at least 1/3.

s.t: A runner is “lonely” if their distance from each of the other runners is at least 1/3.
